scores.notes: The response never assembles a complete runnable charm.py — it presents code only as fragments (helper method, event handler stub) scattered through prose. The __init__ with framework.observe is never shown as actual code, and the full file with imports and ops.main() is absent. The individual pieces shown are correct: socket.getfqdn(), writing to relation.data[self.unit], ops.RelationJoinedEvent type hints, and the charmcraft.yaml provides stanza are all idiomatic and hallucination-free. The idempotency guard and re-publish on config_changed/upgrade_charm are good additions. Deducted on runs_correctly and complete because the question asked for a complete charm class and what was delivered cannot be run without assembly.
